takeaways: Senior living occupancy hit 88.7% in Q3 2025 with independent living surpassing 90% for the first time since 2019 — NIC MAP projects industry-wide 90%+ by year-end 2026, but 549,000 new units are needed by 2028 to meet demand.

The Fed held rates at 3.50–3.75% in March with only one cut expected this year — CRE capital markets are reawakening anyway, with Colliers forecasting 15–20% higher sales volume as institutional investors shift from defense to selective deployment.

Proptech VC funding surged 176% YoY in January 2026 to $1.7B in a single month — agentic AI is moving from pilot projects to mission-critical infrastructure across deal underwriting, building operations, and lease compliance.

items.headline: Senior Living Occupancy Hits 88.7%, Independent Living Breaks 90%
items.summary: NIC MAP data shows senior housing occupancy rose 70 bps to 88.7% in Q3 2025, the 17th consecutive quarterly gain. Independent living surpassed 90% for the first time since 2019. With the oldest boomers turning 80 this year and only 0.7% YoY inventory growth (lowest since 2006), operators have significant pricing power heading into 2026.

items.headline: Senior Housing Needs 549,000 New Units by 2028 as Pipeline Lags
items.summary: NIC MAP projects a need for 549,000 additional senior living units by 2028 and 806,000 by 2030. The average construction cycle has stretched to 29 months, meaning projects breaking ground in early 2026 won't open until 2028. The industry is in the early innings of its next supply cycle, with developers trying to catch up to demand rather than get ahead of it.

items.headline: Fed Holds Rates at 3.50–3.75%, Signals One Cut This Year
items.summary: The FOMC voted 11-1 to hold the federal funds rate steady at 3.50–3.75% in March, citing hotter-than-expected inflation at 2.7% PCE and a softening labor market. The dot plot signals one reduction in 2026 and another in 2027. The 10-Year Treasury has stabilized around 4.26%, providing more predictability for CRE debt pricing.

items.headline: CRE Capital Markets Reawaken with 15–20% Volume Increase Forecast
items.summary: Colliers reports deal velocity is rising and forecasts a 15–20% increase in CRE sales volume in 2026 as institutional and cross-border capital reenters the market. The rolling four-quarter price per unit in senior housing reached $174,000 in Q3 2025, up 43% YoY. Investors are shifting from defense to selective deployment, though lenders remain cautious on marginal deals.

items.headline: Proptech VC Funding Surges 176% YoY in January 2026 to $1.7B
items.summary: Venture capital firms invested approximately $1.7 billion in proptech in January 2026 alone, a 176% increase from January 2025. Full-year 2025 proptech investment hit $16.7B globally, up 67.9% YoY. AI-centered proptech companies grew at 42% annualized vs. 24% for non-AI firms, signaling the sector's AI-first transformation.

items.headline: Agentic AI Moves from Pilots to Mission-Critical CRE Infrastructure
items.summary: ICSC reports agentic AI is reshaping real estate operations in 2026, automating procurement, pricing, inventory management, and event programming. EliseAI raised $250M at a $2.2B valuation, while Bedrock Robotics secured $270M in February 2026. McKinsey notes AI agents are now handling deal underwriting, lease compliance, and building operations at scale.

items.headline: Senior Living Operating Margins Top 25%, Highest Since 2018
items.summary: NIC MAP data shows average senior housing operating margins surpassed 25% in mid-2025, the highest level since 2018, as occupancy and rent growth outpaced expense inflation. Operators are taking a more targeted approach to rate strategies in 2026 — not a reset but an evolution — gaining pricing power as satisfaction scores improve.

items.headline: Erickson, HRA Lead New Senior Living Construction Starts
items.summary: Erickson Senior Living has started construction on a new community in Maryland, while Harbor Retirement Associates broke ground on a 172-unit community in St. Johns, Florida spanning IL, AL, and memory care. All Seasons Delray also started construction on 153 units in South Florida. These projects reflect selective development targeting high-demand Sun Belt and mid-Atlantic markets.

items.headline: Commercial Mortgage Rates Start at 5.39% as Lending Stabilizes
items.summary: As of late March 2026, commercial mortgage rates start at 5.39% with the 10-Year Treasury at approximately 4.26%. While short-term rates have stabilized, lenders remain selective — transactions with strong cash flow and conservative leverage are closing, while marginal deals remain difficult. The improved rate environment is expected to support 2026 transaction volume growth.

items.headline: 4 New Proptech Unicorns Highlight AI's Growing Role in CRE
items.summary: Four new proptech companies have reached unicorn status, underscoring AI's increasing role in commercial real estate. These AI-native companies are directly influencing underwriting accuracy, rent collection, lease compliance, and construction cost control. The next generation of proptech unicorns will be built on AI-first architectures rather than retrofitted legacy platforms.

items.headline: 71% of Leaders Under Increased Stress, 40% Consider Leaving
items.summary: DDI's 2026 leadership report finds 71% of leaders are under increased stress, with 40% considering leaving their roles. U.S. layoffs have surged to their highest point in 10 years, leaving organizations lean. The research highlights that leadership fundamentals — empathy, trust, and emotional intelligence — matter more as uncertainty increases.

items.headline: Strategic Agility Defines Winning Organizations in 2026
items.summary: Aspen Institute and IMD research show the best-performing organizations in 2026 won't be those with the most polished strategies, but those that learn fastest when strategies break. Strategic localization — redesigning operating models so resilience is built closer to where value is created — is emerging as a key competitive advantage for multi-site operators.

items.headline: 89% of Workers Report Better Performance When Prioritizing Wellness
items.summary: Wellhub's 2026 State of Work-Life Wellness report, surveying 5,000+ employees across 10 countries, found 89% report better performance when prioritizing health. Companies integrating well-being into culture see up to 20% higher productivity. However, 90% of employees experienced burnout symptoms in the past year, with 40% experiencing them weekly.

items.headline: AI-Driven Personalized Wellness Reaches Tipping Point in 2026
items.summary: The Global Wellness Summit identifies 2026 as a turning point for personalized wellness powered by AI-driven analytics, at-home biomarker tests, and advanced wearables. A new category of 'emotional fitness' is emerging that emphasizes cognitive performance and emotional resilience — directly relevant for senior living programming and resident engagement strategies.

items.headline: Luxury Senior Living Community Valara Breaks Ground in Henderson, NV
items.summary: Investcor announced a new 244-unit luxury senior living community called Valara in Henderson, Nevada, expected to break ground in Q2 2026. The project targets the growing high-end segment of the senior housing market in the Las Vegas metro, reflecting developer confidence in Sun Belt demand fundamentals.

items.headline: Smart Bricks Raises $5M to Build AI-Native Real Estate Investing Layer
items.summary: Smart Bricks closed a $5 million pre-seed round led by Andreessen Horowitz to build an AI-native infrastructure layer for global real estate investing through agentic AI. The platform aims to automate the entire real estate investment workflow from sourcing to underwriting to asset management.

items.headline: Learning Becomes Top Retention Strategy as Talent Market Tightens
items.summary: Multiple leadership studies confirm that learning and development is no longer a perk but a core retention strategy in 2026. With layoffs at a 10-year high and manager stress elevated, organizations that invest in leadership development and upskilling are better positioned to retain talent through uncertainty.

items.headline: Wellness 'Third Places' Reshape Workplace Relationships
items.summary: Wellness-focused spaces outside home and office — gyms, yoga studios, and wellness hubs — are becoming the new epicenter of workplace relationships. 91% of workers say these 'third places' help them handle work pressures more effectively, signaling an opportunity for senior living operators to integrate wellness community spaces into their programming.
